title: CLI Reference
intro: Command-by-command guide for `@fr4n0m/contract-kit`: what each command does, when to run it, and what you should expect after execution.

commands.name: init
commands.description: Create base contract workspace and starter files.
commands.whenToUse: Run once per repository or when bootstrapping a new API workspace.
outputs: Creates baseline config for contract workflow.
Leaves project ready for first contract file.

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate typed outputs (model, Angular client, backend helpers).
commands.whenToUse: Run after every contract change and before committing code that depends on generated artifacts.
outputs: Refreshes contract model and generated client/backend artifacts.
Keeps frontend and backend code aligned to latest contract.
Provides deterministic files for review and CI.

commands.name: check
commands.description: Detect compatibility changes and report potential breaking changes.
commands.whenToUse: Run before release and in CI pull requests to block unsafe API changes.
outputs: Reports detected breaking and non-breaking changes.
Returns non-zero exit code on breaking changes.

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Run schema validation only, without generating files.
commands.whenToUse: Run in pre-commit hooks or early CI steps for fast contract sanity checks.
outputs: Validates contract shape and required fields.

commands.name: mock
commands.description: Generate deterministic mock payloads from contracts.
commands.whenToUse: Run when you need stable sample payloads for frontend integration or API demos.
outputs: Produces predictable mock payloads per endpoint/status.
Helps frontend teams build against contract before backend is complete.

executionFlow: Define or update contract files under `contracts/`.
tips: Run `validate` in pre-commit or CI for fast guardrails.
Run `check` before release to catch breaking changes early.
Use generated outputs as source of truth in Angular and backend.
